# Test HTTP server performance with ultra-aggressive timeouts

# Set ultra-aggressive environment variables
env MCP_TEST_TIMEOUT=1s
env MCP_SERVER_TIMEOUT=800ms
env MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT=500ms

# Start server and test rapid-fire requests
bg bash 'go run main.go'
sleep 500ms

# Test multiple rapid requests within timeout
bash 'for i in {1..3}; do echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": $i, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" | go run main.go > /dev/null & done; wait'

# Test concurrent tool calls (should complete within aggressive timeout)
bash 'echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"tools/call\", \"params\": {\"name\": \"http_get\", \"arguments\": {\"url\": \"https://httpbin.org/get\"}}}" | timeout 2s go run main.go'
stdout '"status_code"'

# Test server startup time (should be under 1 second)
bash 'start_time=$(date +%s%N); go run main.go <<< "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 1, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" > /dev/null; end_time=$(date +%s%N); duration=$((($end_time - $start_time) / 1000000)); echo "Startup + response time: ${duration}ms"; test $duration -lt 1500'

# Test memory efficiency (quick response)
bash 'echo "{\"jsonrpc\": \"2.0\", \"id\": 2, \"method\": \"tools/list\"}" | timeout 1s go run main.go'
stdout '"tools"'

# Cleanup
exec pkill -f "go run main.go" || true
